prompt:
Function signature you must implement:

```python
def check_permission(user: dict, resource: dict, action: str) -> dict:
    """
    Args:
        user: {
            "id": str,
            "role": "admin" | "reviewer" | "member" | "guest",
            "teams": list[str],
            "revoked_scopes": list[{ "resource_id": str, "action": str }]
        }
        resource: {
            "id": str,
            "owner_id": str,
            "team_id": str
        }
        action: "read" | "write" | "delete"
    Returns:
        {
            "allowed": bool,
            "reason": str  -- one of: "revoked", "admin", "owner",
                "reviewer_read", "member_team", "default_deny"
        }
    """

```

Specification (description of what the function should do):

PermissionChecker
  .input(user: User, resource: Resource, action: Action)
  .sequence(
      // explicit revocations beat everything
      check_revocations(user.revoked_scopes, resource.id, action) -> if_revoked: deny,

      // admin always allowed (except revoked, handled above)
      if_admin(user.role == "admin"): allow,

      // owner allowed on their own stuff (any action)
      if_owner(user.id == resource.owner_id): allow,

      // reviewer: read-only
      if_reviewer(user.role == "reviewer", action == "read"): allow,

      // member: read or write on resources their team owns
      if_member(user.role == "member",
                action in ["read", "write"],
                resource.team_id in user.teams): allow,

      // default deny
      default: deny)
  .output(allowed: bool, reason: str)
